# API Reference[¶](https://app.bluequbit.io/sdk-docs/bluequbit.sdk.html#api-reference "Link to this heading") 

bluequbit.init(_api_token =None_, _execution_mode =None_)[¶](https://app.bluequbit.io/sdk-docs/bluequbit.sdk.html#bluequbit.init "Link to this definition") 
    
Returns [`BQClient`](https://app.bluequbit.io/sdk-docs/bluequbit.sdk.html#bluequbit.BQClient "bluequbit.BQClient") instance for managing jobs on BlueQubit platform. 

Parameters: 
    
  * **api_token** (_str_ _|__None_) – API token of the user. If `None`, the token will be looked in the environment variable BLUEQUBIT_API_TOKEN.
  * **execution_mode** (_str_ _|__None_) – Execution mode. Can be `"cloud"`, `"local"` or `None`. If `None`, the environment variable BLUEQUBIT_EXECUTION_MODE will be used if set, otherwise `"cloud"` will be used.



Return type: 
    
[_BQClient_](https://app.bluequbit.io/sdk-docs/bluequbit.sdk.html#bluequbit.BQClient "bluequbit.bluequbit_client.BQClient")
* * * 

classbluequbit.BQClient(_api_token =None_, _execution_mode =None_)[¶](https://app.bluequbit.io/sdk-docs/bluequbit.sdk.html#bluequbit.BQClient "Link to this definition") 
    
Client for managing jobs on BlueQubit platform. 

Parameters: 
    
  * **api_token** (_str_ _|__None_) – API token of the user. If `None`, the token will be looked in the environment variable BLUEQUBIT_API_TOKEN.
  * **execution_mode** (_str_ _|__None_) – Execution mode. Can be `"cloud"`, `"local"` or `None`. If `None`, the environment variable BLUEQUBIT_EXECUTION_MODE will be used if set, otherwise `"cloud"` will be used.



estimate(_circuits_ , _device ='cpu'_)[¶](https://app.bluequbit.io/sdk-docs/bluequbit.sdk.html#bluequbit.BQClient.estimate "Link to this definition") 
    
Estimate job runtime. Not supported in local execution mode. 

Parameters: 
    
  * **circuits** (_Cirq_ _,__Qiskit_ _,__list_) – quantum circuit or circuits
  * **device** (_str_) – device for which to estimate the circuit. Can be one of `"cpu"` | `"gpu"` | `"quantum"`



Returns: 
    
result or results estimate metadata 

Return type: 
    
[_EstimateResult_](https://app.bluequbit.io/sdk-docs/bluequbit.sdk.html#bluequbit.EstimateResult "bluequbit.estimate_result.EstimateResult") | list[[_EstimateResult_](https://app.bluequbit.io/sdk-docs/bluequbit.sdk.html#bluequbit.EstimateResult "bluequbit.estimate_result.EstimateResult")] 

run(_circuits_ , _device ='cpu'_, _*_ , _asynchronous =False_, _job_name =None_, _shots =None_, _pauli_sum =None_, _options =None_, _tags =None_, _timeout =None_, _force_run =False_)[¶](https://app.bluequbit.io/sdk-docs/bluequbit.sdk.html#bluequbit.BQClient.run "Link to this definition") 
    
Submit a job to run on BlueQubit platform. 

Parameters: 
    
  * **circuits** (_Cirq_ _,__Qiskit_ _,__list_) – quantum circuit or list of circuits
  * **device** (_str_) – device on which to run the circuit. Can be one of `"cpu"` | `"gpu"` | `"quantum"` | `"mps.cpu"` | `"mps.gpu"` | `"pauli-path"`
  * **asynchronous** (_bool_) – if set to `False`, wait for job completion before returning. If set to `True`, return immediately
  * **job_name** (_str_ _|__None_) – customizable job name
  * **shots** (_int_ _|__None_) – number of shots to run. If device is quantum and shots is None then it is set to 1000. For non quantum devices, if None, full probability distribution will be returned. For mps.cpu and mps.gpu devices it is limited to 1000000, for all other devices it is limited to 100000
  * **pauli_sum** (_list_ _[__tuple_ _[__str_ _,__float_ _]__]__|__str_ _|__list_ _[__list_ _[__tuple_ _[__str_ _,__float_ _]__]__|__str_ _]__|__None_) – The Pauli sum or a list of Pauli sum which expectation value is the computation result
  * **options** (_dict_ _|__None_) – 
dictionary of options used to configure the run:
    * `mps_bond_dimension` (int): Sets a limit on the number of Schmidt coefficients retained at the end of the svd algorithm. Coefficients beyond this limit will be discarded. Default: None (no limit on the bond dimension). Applicable only to `"mps.cpu"` and `"mps.gpu"` devices.
    * `pauli_path_truncation_threshold` (float): Sets a lower bound on the absolute value of coefficients of the Pauli operators retained after each step of the Pauli-path propagation algorithm. Pauli operators with coefficients less than this threshold will be discarded. Choosing a smaller threshold keeps more Pauli terms in the simulation and provides a better estimate of the expectation value, but at the cost of increased runtime. Smallest value currently supported is 1e-5. For circuits with more than 13 qubits a `pauli_path_truncation_threshold` must be specified. For exact simulation of circuits with <= 13 qubits, set `pauli_path_truncation_threshold` to `None`. Applicable only to `"pauli-path"` device.
    * `pauli_path_circuit_transpilation_level` (int): Controls the extend of transpilation performed while converting the given circuit into one consisting of only rotation gates. Must be one of 0, 1, 2 or 3, with 0 corresponding to minimal tranpilation, whereas level 3 involves extensive, potentially time consuming, transpilation passes with different bases to minimize gate counts. In general, different transpilation levels may result in slightly different expectation values. Default: 2 Applicable only to `"pauli-path"` device
  * **tags** (_dict_ _[__str_ _,__Any_ _]__|__None_) – an optional dictionary containing key-value pairs to help tag the job.
  * **timeout** (_float_ _|__None_) – maximum wait time in seconds when `asynchronous` is `False`. If the job does not complete within the specified time, an exception will be raised. If None, the wait will continue indefinitely.
  * **force_run** (_bool_) – if `True`, bypass server-side limitations in case small allowed_runtime force the job to execute. Applicable only to `"mps.cpu"` and `"mps.gpu"` devices. Default: `False`.



Returns: 
    
job or jobs metadata 

Return type: 
    
[_JobResult_](https://app.bluequbit.io/sdk-docs/bluequbit.sdk.html#bluequbit.JobResult "bluequbit.job_result.JobResult") | list[[_JobResult_](https://app.bluequbit.io/sdk-docs/bluequbit.sdk.html#bluequbit.JobResult "bluequbit.job_result.JobResult")] 

wait(_job_ids_ , _timeout =None_)[¶](https://app.bluequbit.io/sdk-docs/bluequbit.sdk.html#bluequbit.BQClient.wait "Link to this definition") 
    
Wait for job completion. Not supported in local execution mode. 

Parameters: 
    
  * **job_ids** (_str_ _|__list_ _[__str_ _]__|_[_JobResult_](https://app.bluequbit.io/sdk-docs/bluequbit.sdk.html#bluequbit.JobResult "bluequbit.job_result.JobResult") _|__list_ _[_[_JobResult_](https://app.bluequbit.io/sdk-docs/bluequbit.sdk.html#bluequbit.JobResult "bluequbit.job_result.JobResult") _]_) – job IDs that can be found as property of [`JobResult`](https://app.bluequbit.io/sdk-docs/bluequbit.sdk.html#bluequbit.JobResult "bluequbit.JobResult") metadata of [`run()`](https://app.bluequbit.io/sdk-docs/bluequbit.sdk.html#bluequbit.BQClient.run "bluequbit.BQClient.run") method, or JobResult instances from which job IDs will be extracted
  * **timeout** (_float_ _|__None_) – maximum wait time in seconds If the job does not complete within the specified time, an exception will be raised. If None, the wait will continue indefinitely. The raised error will have an error.job_ids attribute that can be used to identify the jobs that didn’t complete within the specified time and be canceled.



Returns: 
    
job metadata 

Return type: 
    
[_JobResult_](https://app.bluequbit.io/sdk-docs/bluequbit.sdk.html#bluequbit.JobResult "bluequbit.job_result.JobResult") | list[[_JobResult_](https://app.bluequbit.io/sdk-docs/bluequbit.sdk.html#bluequbit.JobResult "bluequbit.job_result.JobResult")] 

get(_job_ids_)[¶](https://app.bluequbit.io/sdk-docs/bluequbit.sdk.html#bluequbit.BQClient.get "Link to this definition") 
    
Get current metadata of jobs. Not supported in local execution mode. 

Parameters: 
    
**job_ids** (_str_ _|__list_ _[__str_ _]__|_[_JobResult_](https://app.bluequbit.io/sdk-docs/bluequbit.sdk.html#bluequbit.JobResult "bluequbit.job_result.JobResult") _|__list_ _[_[_JobResult_](https://app.bluequbit.io/sdk-docs/bluequbit.sdk.html#bluequbit.JobResult "bluequbit.job_result.JobResult") _]_) – job IDs that can be found as property of [`JobResult`](https://app.bluequbit.io/sdk-docs/bluequbit.sdk.html#bluequbit.JobResult "bluequbit.JobResult") metadata of [`run()`](https://app.bluequbit.io/sdk-docs/bluequbit.sdk.html#bluequbit.BQClient.run "bluequbit.BQClient.run") method 

Returns: 
    
jobs metadata 

Return type: 
    
[_JobResult_](https://app.bluequbit.io/sdk-docs/bluequbit.sdk.html#bluequbit.JobResult "bluequbit.job_result.JobResult") | list[[_JobResult_](https://app.bluequbit.io/sdk-docs/bluequbit.sdk.html#bluequbit.JobResult "bluequbit.job_result.JobResult")] 

cancel(_job_ids_)[¶](https://app.bluequbit.io/sdk-docs/bluequbit.sdk.html#bluequbit.BQClient.cancel "Link to this definition") 
    
Submit jobs cancel request. Not supported in local execution mode. 

Parameters: 
    
**job_ids** (_str_ _|__list_ _[__str_ _]__|_[_JobResult_](https://app.bluequbit.io/sdk-docs/bluequbit.sdk.html#bluequbit.JobResult "bluequbit.job_result.JobResult") _|__list_ _[_[_JobResult_](https://app.bluequbit.io/sdk-docs/bluequbit.sdk.html#bluequbit.JobResult "bluequbit.job_result.JobResult") _]_) – job IDs that can be found as property of [`JobResult`](https://app.bluequbit.io/sdk-docs/bluequbit.sdk.html#bluequbit.JobResult "bluequbit.JobResult") metadata of [`run()`](https://app.bluequbit.io/sdk-docs/bluequbit.sdk.html#bluequbit.BQClient.run "bluequbit.BQClient.run") method 

Returns: 
    
job or jobs metadata 

Return type: 
    
[_JobResult_](https://app.bluequbit.io/sdk-docs/bluequbit.sdk.html#bluequbit.JobResult "bluequbit.job_result.JobResult") | list[[_JobResult_](https://app.bluequbit.io/sdk-docs/bluequbit.sdk.html#bluequbit.JobResult "bluequbit.job_result.JobResult")] 

search(_run_status =None_, _created_later_than =None_, _batch_id =None_)[¶](https://app.bluequbit.io/sdk-docs/bluequbit.sdk.html#bluequbit.BQClient.search "Link to this definition") 
    
Search jobs. Not supported in local execution mode. 

Parameters: 
    
  * **run_status** (_str_ _|__None_) – if not `None`, run status of jobs to filter. Can be one of `"FAILED_VALIDATION"` | `"PENDING"` | `"QUEUED"` | `"RUNNING"` | `"TERMINATED"` | `"CANCELED"` | `"NOT_ENOUGH_FUNDS"` | `"COMPLETED"`
  * **created_later_than** (_str_ _|__datetime.datetime_ _|__None_) – if not `None`, filter by latest job creation datetime. Please add timezone for clarity, otherwise UTC will be assumed
  * **batch_id** (_str_ _|__None_) – if not `None`, filter by batch ID



Returns: 
    
metadata of jobs 

Return type: 
    
list[[JobResult](https://app.bluequbit.io/sdk-docs/bluequbit.sdk.html#bluequbit.JobResult "bluequbit.JobResult")] 

asyncrun_native_async(_* args_, _polling_interval =1.0_, _** kwargs_)[¶](https://app.bluequbit.io/sdk-docs/bluequbit.sdk.html#bluequbit.BQClient.run_native_async "Link to this definition") 
    
Experimental: Submit a job to run on BlueQubit platform with asyncio. Not supported in local execution mode. 

Parameters: 
    
  * **args** – arguments for the [`run()`](https://app.bluequbit.io/sdk-docs/bluequbit.sdk.html#bluequbit.BQClient.run "bluequbit.BQClient.run") method
  * **polling_interval** (_float_) – interval in seconds to poll for job completion
  * **kwargs** – keyword arguments for the [`run()`](https://app.bluequbit.io/sdk-docs/bluequbit.sdk.html#bluequbit.BQClient.run "bluequbit.BQClient.run") method



Returns: 
    
job or jobs metadata 

Return type: 
    
[JobResult](https://app.bluequbit.io/sdk-docs/bluequbit.sdk.html#bluequbit.JobResult "bluequbit.JobResult") or list[[JobResult](https://app.bluequbit.io/sdk-docs/bluequbit.sdk.html#bluequbit.JobResult "bluequbit.JobResult")]
* * * 

classbluequbit.JobResult(_data_ , __backend_connection =None_)[¶](https://app.bluequbit.io/sdk-docs/bluequbit.sdk.html#bluequbit.JobResult "Link to this definition") 
    
This class contains information from a job run on quantum hardware or quantum simulators. Mainly it contains the resulting statevector from the run. It might contain only partial information, such as job ID, when [`BQClient.run()`](https://app.bluequbit.io/sdk-docs/bluequbit.sdk.html#bluequbit.BQClient.run "bluequbit.BQClient.run") is called with `asynchronous=True`. 

job_id[¶](https://app.bluequbit.io/sdk-docs/bluequbit.sdk.html#bluequbit.JobResult.job_id "Link to this definition") 
    
str: job id 

batch_id[¶](https://app.bluequbit.io/sdk-docs/bluequbit.sdk.html#bluequbit.JobResult.batch_id "Link to this definition") 
    
str: batch id 

job_name[¶](https://app.bluequbit.io/sdk-docs/bluequbit.sdk.html#bluequbit.JobResult.job_name "Link to this definition") 
    
str: job name 

device[¶](https://app.bluequbit.io/sdk-docs/bluequbit.sdk.html#bluequbit.JobResult.device "Link to this definition") 
    
str: run device 

device_options[¶](https://app.bluequbit.io/sdk-docs/bluequbit.sdk.html#bluequbit.JobResult.device_options "Link to this definition") 
    
dict: device parameters used in circuit execution 

estimated_runtime[¶](https://app.bluequbit.io/sdk-docs/bluequbit.sdk.html#bluequbit.JobResult.estimated_runtime "Link to this definition") 
    
int: estimated runtime in milliseconds 

estimated_cost[¶](https://app.bluequbit.io/sdk-docs/bluequbit.sdk.html#bluequbit.JobResult.estimated_cost "Link to this definition") 
    
float: estimated cost in US dollars 

created_on[¶](https://app.bluequbit.io/sdk-docs/bluequbit.sdk.html#bluequbit.JobResult.created_on "Link to this definition") 
    
datetime: job creation date in UTC timezone 

top_128_results[¶](https://app.bluequbit.io/sdk-docs/bluequbit.sdk.html#bluequbit.JobResult.top_128_results "Link to this definition") 
    
dict: top 128 results 

num_qubits[¶](https://app.bluequbit.io/sdk-docs/bluequbit.sdk.html#bluequbit.JobResult.num_qubits "Link to this definition") 
    
int: number of qubits 

num_qubits_used[¶](https://app.bluequbit.io/sdk-docs/bluequbit.sdk.html#bluequbit.JobResult.num_qubits_used "Link to this definition") 
    
int: number of used qubits 

tags[¶](https://app.bluequbit.io/sdk-docs/bluequbit.sdk.html#bluequbit.JobResult.tags "Link to this definition") 
    
dict[str, Any]: tags, a JSON serializable dictionary. 

pauli_sum[¶](https://app.bluequbit.io/sdk-docs/bluequbit.sdk.html#bluequbit.JobResult.pauli_sum "Link to this definition") 
    
list[tuple[str, float]]: expectation value 

expectation_value[¶](https://app.bluequbit.io/sdk-docs/bluequbit.sdk.html#bluequbit.JobResult.expectation_value "Link to this definition") 
    
float | list[float]: expectation value 

queue_time_ms[¶](https://app.bluequbit.io/sdk-docs/bluequbit.sdk.html#bluequbit.JobResult.queue_time_ms "Link to this definition") 
    
int: job queue time in milliseconds 

run_time_ms[¶](https://app.bluequbit.io/sdk-docs/bluequbit.sdk.html#bluequbit.JobResult.run_time_ms "Link to this definition") 
    
int: job runtime in milliseconds 

error_message[¶](https://app.bluequbit.io/sdk-docs/bluequbit.sdk.html#bluequbit.JobResult.error_message "Link to this definition") 
    
str: error message if failed 

cost[¶](https://app.bluequbit.io/sdk-docs/bluequbit.sdk.html#bluequbit.JobResult.cost "Link to this definition") 
    
float: job cost in US dollars 

has_statevector[¶](https://app.bluequbit.io/sdk-docs/bluequbit.sdk.html#bluequbit.JobResult.has_statevector "Link to this definition") 
    
bool: if statevector available 

circuit[¶](https://app.bluequbit.io/sdk-docs/bluequbit.sdk.html#bluequbit.JobResult.circuit "Link to this definition") 
    
Cirq, Qiskit, dict: original circuit 

shots[¶](https://app.bluequbit.io/sdk-docs/bluequbit.sdk.html#bluequbit.JobResult.shots "Link to this definition") 
    
int: number of shots specified during the job submission 

run_results[¶](https://app.bluequbit.io/sdk-docs/bluequbit.sdk.html#bluequbit.JobResult.run_results "Link to this definition") 
    
dict | None: additional results from the job execution (e.g., mps_build_time) 

get_statevector()[¶](https://app.bluequbit.io/sdk-docs/bluequbit.sdk.html#bluequbit.JobResult.get_statevector "Link to this definition") 
    
Return statevector of the job. If the statevector is too large then throws exception. 

Return type: 
    
NumPy array 

get_counts()[¶](https://app.bluequbit.io/sdk-docs/bluequbit.sdk.html#bluequbit.JobResult.get_counts "Link to this definition") 
    
If shots argument in bq.run() was not None, return a number of samples from the measurements, otherwise, measurement probabilities of the computation result, without any statistical fluctuation are returned. In both cases only top 131072 (2^17) results are returned. 

Return type: 
    
None | dict[str, float] 

propertyok:bool[¶](https://app.bluequbit.io/sdk-docs/bluequbit.sdk.html#bluequbit.JobResult.ok "Link to this definition") 
    
`True`, if job’s current run status is `"COMPLETED"`, else `False`.
* * * 

classbluequbit.EstimateResult(_data_)[¶](https://app.bluequbit.io/sdk-docs/bluequbit.sdk.html#bluequbit.EstimateResult "Link to this definition") 
    
This class contains information about the estimated runtime/cost that will be incurred from running the given circuit on the given quantum machine/simulator. WARNING: this is just an estimate, the actual runtime/cost may be less or more. 

estimated_runtime[¶](https://app.bluequbit.io/sdk-docs/bluequbit.sdk.html#bluequbit.EstimateResult.estimated_runtime "Link to this definition") 
    
int: estimated runtime in milliseconds 

estimated_cost[¶](https://app.bluequbit.io/sdk-docs/bluequbit.sdk.html#bluequbit.EstimateResult.estimated_cost "Link to this definition") 
    
float: estimated cost in US dollars 

num_qubits[¶](https://app.bluequbit.io/sdk-docs/bluequbit.sdk.html#bluequbit.EstimateResult.num_qubits "Link to this definition") 
    
int: number of qubits 

warning_message[¶](https://app.bluequbit.io/sdk-docs/bluequbit.sdk.html#bluequbit.EstimateResult.warning_message "Link to this definition") 
    
str: warning message 

error_message[¶](https://app.bluequbit.io/sdk-docs/bluequbit.sdk.html#bluequbit.EstimateResult.error_message "Link to this definition") 
    
str: error message if available
* * * 

classbluequbit.BlueQubitProvider(_*_ , _api_token =None_, _backend =None_)[¶](https://app.bluequbit.io/sdk-docs/bluequbit.sdk.html#bluequbit.BlueQubitProvider "Link to this definition") 
    
A Qiskit provider for accessing BlueQubit backend. 

Parameters: 
    
  * **api_token** (_str_ _|__None_) – API token of the user. If `None`, the token will be looked in the environment variable BLUEQUBIT_API_TOKEN.
  * **backend** (_BlueQubitBackendV2_ _|__None_) – BlueQubit backend to use. If `None`, a new backend will be created using the API token.



get_backend(_execution_mode =None_)[¶](https://app.bluequbit.io/sdk-docs/bluequbit.sdk.html#bluequbit.BlueQubitProvider.get_backend "Link to this definition") 
     

Parameters: 
    
**execution_mode** (_str_ _|__None_) – Execution mode. Can be `"cloud"`, `"local"` or `None`. If `None`, the environment variable BLUEQUBIT_EXECUTION_MODE will be used if set, otherwise `"cloud"` will be used.
* * *
## Exceptions[¶](https://app.bluequbit.io/sdk-docs/bluequbit.sdk.html#exceptions "Link to this heading")
All exceptions reside in `bluequbit.exceptions` module and inherit from base `bluequbit.exceptions.BQError` class.
  * `BQJobInvalidDeviceTypeError`
  * `BQJobNotCompleteError`
  * `BQJobStatevectorTooLargeError`
  * `BQJobHasNotFinishedYetError`
  * `BQJobDoesNotContainCountsError`
  * `BQJobCouldNotCancelError`
  * `BQAPIError`
  * `BQUnauthorizedAccessError`

API Reference¶

bluequbit.init(api_token=None, execution_mode=None)¶

Returns BQClient instance for managing jobs on BlueQubit platform.

Parameters:

  • api_token (str | None) – API token of the user. If None, the token will be looked in the environment variable BLUEQUBIT_API_TOKEN.

  • execution_mode (str | None) – Execution mode. Can be "cloud", "local" or None. If None, the environment variable BLUEQUBIT_EXECUTION_MODE will be used if set, otherwise "cloud" will be used.

Return type:

BQClient

class bluequbit.BQClient(api_token=None, execution_mode=None)¶

Client for managing jobs on BlueQubit platform.

Parameters:

  • api_token (str | None) – API token of the user. If None, the token will be looked in the environment variable BLUEQUBIT_API_TOKEN.

  • execution_mode (str | None) – Execution mode. Can be "cloud", "local" or None. If None, the environment variable BLUEQUBIT_EXECUTION_MODE will be used if set, otherwise "cloud" will be used.

estimate(circuits, device='cpu')¶

Estimate job runtime. Not supported in local execution mode.

Parameters:

  • circuits (Cirq, Qiskit, list) – quantum circuit or circuits

  • device (str) – device for which to estimate the circuit. Can be one of "cpu" | "gpu" | "quantum"

Returns:

result or results estimate metadata

Return type:

EstimateResult | list[EstimateResult]

run(circuits, device='cpu', *, asynchronous=False, job_name=None, shots=None, pauli_sum=None, options=None, tags=None, timeout=None, force_run=False)¶

Submit a job to run on BlueQubit platform.

Parameters:

  • circuits (Cirq, Qiskit, list) – quantum circuit or list of circuits

  • device (str) – device on which to run the circuit. Can be one of "cpu" | "gpu" | "quantum" | "mps.cpu" | "mps.gpu" | "pauli-path"

  • asynchronous (bool) – if set to False, wait for job completion before returning. If set to True, return immediately

  • job_name (str | None) – customizable job name

  • shots (int | None) – number of shots to run. If device is quantum and shots is None then it is set to 1000. For non quantum devices, if None, full probability distribution will be returned. For mps.cpu and mps.gpu devices it is limited to 1000000, for all other devices it is limited to 100000

  • pauli_sum (list[tuple[str, float]] | str | list[list[tuple[str, float]] | str] | None) – The Pauli sum or a list of Pauli sum which expectation value is the computation result

  • options (dict | None) –

    dictionary of options used to configure the run:

    • mps_bond_dimension (int): Sets a limit on the number of Schmidt coefficients retained at the end of the svd algorithm. Coefficients beyond this limit will be discarded. Default: None (no limit on the bond dimension). Applicable only to "mps.cpu" and "mps.gpu" devices.

    • pauli_path_truncation_threshold (float): Sets a lower bound on the absolute value of coefficients of the Pauli operators retained after each step of the Pauli-path propagation algorithm. Pauli operators with coefficients less than this threshold will be discarded. Choosing a smaller threshold keeps more Pauli terms in the simulation and provides a better estimate of the expectation value, but at the cost of increased runtime. Smallest value currently supported is 1e-5. For circuits with more than 13 qubits a pauli_path_truncation_threshold must be specified. For exact simulation of circuits with <= 13 qubits, set pauli_path_truncation_threshold to None. Applicable only to "pauli-path" device.

    • pauli_path_circuit_transpilation_level (int): Controls the extend of transpilation performed while converting the given circuit into one consisting of only rotation gates. Must be one of 0, 1, 2 or 3, with 0 corresponding to minimal tranpilation, whereas level 3 involves extensive, potentially time consuming, transpilation passes with different bases to minimize gate counts. In general, different transpilation levels may result in slightly different expectation values. Default: 2 Applicable only to "pauli-path" device

  • tags (dict[str, Any] | None) – an optional dictionary containing key-value pairs to help tag the job.

  • timeout (float | None) – maximum wait time in seconds when asynchronous is False. If the job does not complete within the specified time, an exception will be raised. If None, the wait will continue indefinitely.

  • force_run (bool) – if True, bypass server-side limitations in case small allowed_runtime force the job to execute. Applicable only to "mps.cpu" and "mps.gpu" devices. Default: False.

Returns:

job or jobs metadata

Return type:

JobResult | list[JobResult]

wait(job_ids, timeout=None)¶

Wait for job completion. Not supported in local execution mode.

Parameters:

  • job_ids (str | list[str] | JobResult | list[JobResult]) – job IDs that can be found as property of JobResult metadata of run() method, or JobResult instances from which job IDs will be extracted

  • timeout (float | None) – maximum wait time in seconds If the job does not complete within the specified time, an exception will be raised. If None, the wait will continue indefinitely. The raised error will have an error.job_ids attribute that can be used to identify the jobs that didn’t complete within the specified time and be canceled.

Returns:

job metadata

Return type:

JobResult | list[JobResult]

get(job_ids)¶

Get current metadata of jobs. Not supported in local execution mode.

Parameters:

job_ids (str | list[str] | JobResult | list[JobResult]) – job IDs that can be found as property of JobResult metadata of run() method

Returns:

jobs metadata

Return type:

JobResult | list[JobResult]

cancel(job_ids)¶

Submit jobs cancel request. Not supported in local execution mode.

Parameters:

job_ids (str | list[str] | JobResult | list[JobResult]) – job IDs that can be found as property of JobResult metadata of run() method

Returns:

job or jobs metadata

Return type:

JobResult | list[JobResult]

search(run_status=None, created_later_than=None, batch_id=None)¶

Search jobs. Not supported in local execution mode.

Parameters:

  • run_status (str | None) – if not None, run status of jobs to filter. Can be one of "FAILED_VALIDATION" | "PENDING" | "QUEUED" | "RUNNING" | "TERMINATED" | "CANCELED" | "NOT_ENOUGH_FUNDS" | "COMPLETED"

  • created_later_than (str | datetime.datetime | None) – if not None, filter by latest job creation datetime. Please add timezone for clarity, otherwise UTC will be assumed

  • batch_id (str | None) – if not None, filter by batch ID

Returns:

metadata of jobs

Return type:

list[JobResult]

async run_native_async(*args, polling_interval=1.0, **kwargs)¶

Experimental: Submit a job to run on BlueQubit platform with asyncio. Not supported in local execution mode.

Parameters:

  • args – arguments for the run() method

  • polling_interval (float) – interval in seconds to poll for job completion

  • kwargs – keyword arguments for the run() method

Returns:

job or jobs metadata

Return type:

JobResult or list[JobResult]

class bluequbit.JobResult(data, _backend_connection=None)¶

This class contains information from a job run on quantum hardware or quantum simulators. Mainly it contains the resulting statevector from the run. It might contain only partial information, such as job ID, when BQClient.run() is called with asynchronous=True.

job_id¶

str: job id

batch_id¶

str: batch id

job_name¶

str: job name

device¶

str: run device

device_options¶

dict: device parameters used in circuit execution

estimated_runtime¶

int: estimated runtime in milliseconds

estimated_cost¶

float: estimated cost in US dollars

created_on¶

datetime: job creation date in UTC timezone

top_128_results¶

dict: top 128 results

num_qubits¶

int: number of qubits

num_qubits_used¶

int: number of used qubits

tags¶

dict[str, Any]: tags, a JSON serializable dictionary.

pauli_sum¶

list[tuple[str, float]]: expectation value

expectation_value¶

float | list[float]: expectation value

queue_time_ms¶

int: job queue time in milliseconds

run_time_ms¶

int: job runtime in milliseconds

error_message¶

str: error message if failed

cost¶

float: job cost in US dollars

has_statevector¶

bool: if statevector available

circuit¶

Cirq, Qiskit, dict: original circuit

shots¶

int: number of shots specified during the job submission

run_results¶

dict | None: additional results from the job execution (e.g., mps_build_time)

get_statevector()¶

Return statevector of the job. If the statevector is too large then throws exception.

Return type:

NumPy array

get_counts()¶

If shots argument in bq.run() was not None, return a number of samples from the measurements, otherwise, measurement probabilities of the computation result, without any statistical fluctuation are returned. In both cases only top 131072 (2^17) results are returned.

Return type:

None | dict[str, float]

property ok: bool¶

True, if job’s current run status is "COMPLETED", else False.

class bluequbit.EstimateResult(data)¶

This class contains information about the estimated runtime/cost that will be incurred from running the given circuit on the given quantum machine/simulator. WARNING: this is just an estimate, the actual runtime/cost may be less or more.

estimated_runtime¶

int: estimated runtime in milliseconds

estimated_cost¶

float: estimated cost in US dollars

num_qubits¶

int: number of qubits

warning_message¶

str: warning message

error_message¶

str: error message if available

class bluequbit.BlueQubitProvider(*, api_token=None, backend=None)¶

A Qiskit provider for accessing BlueQubit backend.

Parameters:

  • api_token (str | None) – API token of the user. If None, the token will be looked in the environment variable BLUEQUBIT_API_TOKEN.

  • backend (BlueQubitBackendV2 | None) – BlueQubit backend to use. If None, a new backend will be created using the API token.

get_backend(execution_mode=None)¶
Parameters:

execution_mode (str | None) – Execution mode. Can be "cloud", "local" or None. If None, the environment variable BLUEQUBIT_EXECUTION_MODE will be used if set, otherwise "cloud" will be used.

Exceptions¶

All exceptions reside in bluequbit.exceptions module and inherit from base bluequbit.exceptions.BQError class.

  • BQJobInvalidDeviceTypeError

  • BQJobNotCompleteError

  • BQJobStatevectorTooLargeError

  • BQJobHasNotFinishedYetError

  • BQJobDoesNotContainCountsError

  • BQJobCouldNotCancelError

  • BQAPIError

  • BQUnauthorizedAccessError
